NOTICE OF CORRECTED FILINGS SUPPORTING EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER GRANTING MANDATORY STAY OF ENFORCEMENT OF JUDGMENT UNDER CIV. PROC. CODE 1710.50(A) (TRANSACTION ID # 78600384) FILED BY DEFENDANT MOUKHAIBER, MARCO , ALSO KNOWN AS ALWAYSMARCO, ALWAYS MARCO, ALWAYSMARCOLOL AND, ALWAYS MARCO LOL

REMARK: 1.1 HEARING HELD CORRECTED NOTICE OF EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER GRANTING MANDATORY STAY OF ENFORCEMENT OF JUDGMENT UNDER CIV. PROC. CODE 1710.50(A). THE MOVING PARTY'S EX PARTE APPLICATION OF RELIEF IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO ESTABLISH EXIGENCY WARRANTING EMERGENCY EX PARTE RELIEF DEFENDANT HAD PLENTY OF TIME TO SEEK RELIEF BY NOTICE MOTION, DEFENDANTS' DELAY DOES NOT CREATE CIRCUMSTANCES JUSTIFYING REVIEW AND RELIEF BY EX PARTE APPLICATION. THE COURT FURTHER FINDS THAT THE REQUESTED RELIEF IS NOT MERITED UNDER 1710.50. THERE IS NO REQUIREMENT FOR A STAY OF ENFORCEMENT IN THIS CONTEXT. UNDER 1710.50(A)(1) THERE IS NO APPEAL FROM THE SISTER-STATE JUDGMENT, AND AN APPEAL FROM AN ORDER DENYING A MOTION TO VACATE A JUDGMENT DOES NOT CONSTITUTE AN APPEAL FROM THE JUDGMENT UNDER 1710.50 (A) (1)......

REMARK: 1.2 THE MOVING PARTY HAS FAILED TO CARRY THE BURDEN THAT THE MONEY JUDGMENT WAS OBTAINED AGAINST A PERSON OR ENTITY FOR EXERCISING A RIGHT GUARANTEED UNDER THE UNITED STATES CONSTITUTION OR THE CALIFORNIA CONSTITUTION. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT THERE IS NO CONSTITUTIONAL RIGHT TO ENGAGE IN DEFAMATION OR THE INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT FINDS THAT A STAY IS NOT REQUIRED OR WARRANTED UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1710.50 (A) (4) THE MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SHOW THAT OTHER CIRCUMSTANCES WHERE THE INTEREST OF JUSTICE REQUIRE STAY OF ENFORCEMENT.
